result:
**Goal: Add an "auto-discovery" mode that autonomously mutates cellular automaton rules and initial conditions, scores each configuration by visual complexity (entropy, symmetry, periodicity detection), and curates a gallery of the most "interesting" emergent patterns it finds — an automated explorer for beautiful simulations.**

This is strategic because the project has 130+ modes but no way to programmatically search the vast parameter space for visually striking behavior. Users currently have to manually tweak rules and hope for the best. An auto-discovery engine would surface hidden gems across the rule space, acting like a tireless curator that breeds and evolves configurations toward maximum visual interest. It builds naturally on the existing evolution lab, rule editor, and analytics infrastructure.

Shall I implement this?
